metadata.title: Simulate Least-Privilege Permission Design for a File Agent with Claude
metadata.note: CLI video. Exclusions: Cowork UI setup, connector configuration, computer-use session management.

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

lines: Least Privilege Before the Agent Touches a File.

beats.role_note: the problem, context, stakes -- BEFORE the CLI opens
beats.narration_text: You need to clean a folder of quarterly reports. The blast radius of pointing the agent at your full Documents directory is everything you have ever written. The working folder is the permission boundary -- setting it before the agent starts is the whole mitigation.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- title card: THE WORKING FOLDER IS THE PERMISSION BOUNDARY. Set it before the agent starts.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: You need to clean a folder of quarterly reports. The blast radius of pointing the agent at your full Documents directory is everything you have ever written. Th

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude to write cowork_prep.py -- classify every file as needed, forbidden, or uncertain, copy only needed files to a working subdirectory, and flag uncertain ones for human review.

props.command: claude "Write cowork_prep.py: given a source directory and task description, (1) list all files, (2) classify each as needed/forbidden/uncertain by filename pattern, (3) copy only needed files to /cowork-working/, (4) print a before/after manifest and flag uncertain files for human review."

beats.narration_text: cowork_prep.py classifies by filename pattern: forbidden patterns include dot-env, credentials, and key files. Needed patterns are task-specific. Everything else is uncertain.

props.code:
import os, shutil, pathlib
FORBIDDEN = [".env","credentials",".key","secret","password"]
NEEDED = [".csv",".xlsx","report","quarterly"]
def classify(fname):
    fl = fname.lower()
    if any(p in fl for p in FORBIDDEN): return "FORBIDDEN"
    if any(p in fl for p in NEEDED):   return "NEEDED"
    return "UNCERTAIN"
def prep(src, dest="cowork-working"):
    pathlib.Path(dest).mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    for f in os.listdir(src):
        c = classify(f)
        if c == "NEEDED": shutil.copy(f"{src}/{f}", dest)
        print(f"{c:<12} {f}")
prep("source/")

beats.narration_text: Before-after manifest prints. Quarterly report files show NEEDED in green. A contract file and a dot-env file show FORBIDDEN in red.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- screen-recording: terminal showing cowork_prep.py output with manifest: Q3-report.csv=NEEDED, contracts.pdf=FORBIDDEN, .env=FORBIDDEN in red, notes.txt=UNCERTAIN in yellow

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Before-after manifest prints. Quarterly report files show NEEDED in green. A contract file and a dot-env file show FORBIDDEN in red.

beats.narration_text: Add the dot-env pattern to the forbidden list explicitly and verify it is flagged as forbidden credentials regardless of the task description.

props.command: claude "Add .env pattern to the forbidden list regardless of task description -- verify it is flagged as FORBIDDEN CREDENTIALS in the manifest output."

beats.narration_text: Revised manifest: dot-env shows FORBIDDEN CREDENTIALS in red, labeled explicitly. The pattern fires before any task classification logic runs.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- screen-recording: terminal showing revised manifest with .env row labeled FORBIDDEN CREDENTIALS in red, flagged as hardcoded exclusion independent of task description

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Revised manifest: dot-env shows FORBIDDEN CREDENTIALS in red, labeled explicitly. The pattern fires before any task classification logic runs.

beats.narration_text: The working folder is the permission boundary. Setting it before the agent starts is the difference between a narrow task and handing a contractor your entire office.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- recap card: WORKING FOLDER = PERMISSION BOUNDARY. Narrow it before the agent starts.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The working folder is the permission boundary. Setting it before the agent starts is the difference between a narrow task and handing a contractor your entire o

beats.narration_text: Before any file-handling agent task, run cowork_prep.py on your source directory. Never point an agent at a directory larger than the task requires.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- next-steps card: Run cowork_prep.py before every file task. Never point the agent at a directory larger than the task.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Before any file-handling agent task, run cowork_prep.py on your source directory. Never point an agent at a directory larger than the task requires.

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
